harnessProfile.description: Self-contained root instruction surface plus source, tests, metric schema, and token-economics record fixtures.
harnessProfile.name: minimal

limitations: Scratch fixture based on committed HarnessForge, not an external real repo.
Injected defects were not named in the prompt, but this is not a benchmark-held-out issue.
Profile roots are ignored scratch copies, not long-lived target repositories.
Codex JSONL did not emit the configured model name in this run.
Prompt/cache state from nearby runs may affect cached input buckets.
Raw JSONL contains local working-directory output and remains ignored under .harnessforge/.

mechanism.name: verification_feedback
mechanism.notes: Prompt did not name the injected defects; target verification first exposed two focused test failures. Runner used per-run scratch HOME and CODEX_HOME, symlinked auth only, ignore-user-config, ignore-rules, disabled hooks/plugins/memories/apps/multi_agent, and workspace-write sandboxing.
mechanism.risk: Short startup guidance may require more source/test rediscovery before repair.

task.description: Fix the failing token-economics tests in this HarnessForge scratch copy. Follow the repository startup path for the current profile. Start by running PYTHONPATH=src:. python3 -m unittest tests.test_token_economics to observe the failure, then make the smallest source-only Python standard-library change needed. Do not edit tests or docs unless the failure proves they are wrong. Rerun the same focused test command. Do not commit or push. Final answer: one sentence with the changed file and verification result.
